content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master` and read the bead-export implementation and CLI wiring. I found 2 fix-now issues: the export flow does not persist a usable mapping from plan bead refs to live br IDs, which breaks downstream milestone execution, and workstream epics are not uniquely identifiable, so duplicate workstream names break idempotent re-export.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The new export path drops the only proposal_id -> actual br bead_id mapping, so downstream milestone execution still cannot recognize exported beads. `materialize_beads()` builds `proposal_id_map` only in memory and never persists it; the returned/reportable state is reduced to flat ID lists (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1569-1571`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1697-1702`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1765-1770`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3932-3989`). But the controller and CLI continue to decide milestone membership by comparing `br ready` / `br show` IDs against the plan refs from `plan.json` (`src/cli/run.rs:294-333`, `src/cli/run.rs:452-464`, `src/cli/run.rs:721-744`, `src/cli/milestone.rs:1530-1585`). Because `br create` generates repository issue IDs instead of the plan proposal refs, a successful export leaves `milestone next`/`milestone run` with zero matching ready beads. remediation_hint: Persist a durable proposal-ref -> live-bead-id mapping that downstream lookups read, or create beads under stable plan IDs so runtime membership checks can resolve exported beads back to the plan.
   Rationale: This is an end-to-end correctness break in the new feature: exporting beads succeeds, but the rest of the milestone workflow still keys off plan IDs and therefore cannot act on the exported backlog.
2. finding_summary: Idempotent export is broken for valid bundles with duplicate workstream names, because workstream epics have no stable per-workstream identity. They are created with only the milestone label and no disambiguator (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1598-1614`), while the reuse path treats multiple active same-title matches as a hard error (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2023-2055`). The bundle model already allows duplicate workstream names and renders them with ordinal display labels (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1283-1309`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:3273-3300`), so a plan can export successfully once and then fail on the required second run. remediation_hint: Give each workstream epic a deterministic identity for reuse, analogous to `proposal:` labels for task beads, so repeated exports can distinguish same-title workstreams.
   Rationale: This violates the stated idempotency requirement on a bundle shape that the planner already considers valid.
